format_discipline.notes: No frontmatter (first line is H1 title). Sidecar reading.meta.json present and valid (word_count, estimated_read_minutes, citations_used, sections). Exactly six sections in order: Overview, Key Concepts, Worked Example, In Practice, Key Takeaways, References.

length.notes: DEEP profile. 1825 words in range; ~7.9 min aloud at 230 wpm consistent with deep band.

citations.notes: [1][2][3] all used inline; bibliography lists all three; no orphan markers, no orphan entries, no fabricated markers.

forward_references.notes: Confidence intervals, train/validation/test split (line 54) and K-fold cross-validation (line 69) appear as bare names with you-will-meet-later pointers only, unbolded and undefined. Correct mention-and-defer.

faithfulness.notes: Sampled assertions (metric-choice table, 30-100 example sizing, held-out/representative, threshold-by-stakes, both worked sketches with recall>=0.90 and accuracy+calibration) all trace to corpus. No new claims or numbers.

beginner_readability.notes: Terms (evaluation plan, metric, test set, labeled, held-out, representative, pass/fail threshold) defined in plain language before use. Concrete-first, short paragraphs, no jargon dumps, no academic hedging.

formatting.notes: Metric-choice table, threshold-by-stakes table, 6-step procedure, two worked sketches all present. Inline examples beside concepts (spam, 5-ticket noise). No walls of text (<=4 sentence paragraphs).

scope.notes: Synthesis topic: reuse of 8.3/8.4/8.5/8.6 metrics to GUIDE metric choice is on-headline, not drift. No teaching beyond the title; advanced statistics name-and-deferred only. No scope_creep.

diagram_embed.notes: Diagram embedded inline at line 17 with descriptive alt text and caption. PNG existence confirmed by orchestrator via long-path; not raising png-absent.

verdict: PASS. Reading 8.7 meets the DEEP bar: 1825 words in range, faithful to corpus, citations [1][2][3] clean, beginner tone and scannable formatting (two tables, 6-step procedure, two worked sketches), diagram embedded inline. Advanced statistics (confidence intervals, train/validation/test split, K-fold cross-validation) are name-and-deferred only; cross-topic metric reuse is correct synthesis, not scope creep.
